Implements the SDK home application for Pebble firmware, managing UI updates and app interactions.

# Purpose
The code is a C source file for a Pebble smartwatch application. It defines the main functionality of an application that serves as a home screen or launcher interface. The application manages the display of time and provides user interface elements to interact with installed applications and system features like the timeline. The code includes functions to update the user interface, handle button clicks, and manage application state. It uses several components from the Pebble SDK, such as `Window`, `TextLayer`, and various system services for managing applications and displaying information.

The `SdkAppData` structure is central to the application, holding the main window and text layers for displaying information. The code includes conditional compilation directives to support different capabilities, such as `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4`, which affects how the time and information are displayed. The [`prv_init`](<#prv_init>) function initializes the application, setting up the window, configuring button click handlers, and subscribing to time updates. The [`s_main`](<#s_main>) function serves as the entry point, initializing the application and entering the event loop. The [`sdk_app_get_info`](<#sdk_app_get_info>) function provides metadata about the application, including its UUID and name.

# Data Structures

---
### SdkAppData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: Holds the main application window.
    - ``info_text_layer``: Displays informational text to the user.
    - ``time_text_layer``: Displays the current time (only if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined).
    - ``time_buffer``: Stores the formatted time string (only if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined).
- **Description**: `SdkAppData` is a structure that contains elements necessary for managing the user interface of an application, including a window and text layers for displaying information and time. The presence of certain members depends on the compilation condition `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4`, which adds additional functionality for time display.


# Functions

---
### prv\_sdk\_home\_update\_proc<!-- {{#callable:prv_sdk_home_update_proc}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L45>)

Updates the display layer by drawing a black line below the time text layer if the `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` object that needs updating.
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` object used for drawing operations.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`window_do_layer_update_proc`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_do_layer_update_proc>) to perform the default layer update.
    - Checks if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined to conditionally execute the following block.
    - Retrieves user data using `app_state_get_user_data` and calculates the maximum y-coordinate of the `time_text_layer`.
    - Sets the fill color to black using `graphics_context_set_fill_color`.
    - Draws a black rectangle below the time text layer using `graphics_fill_rect`.
- **Output**: No return value; it updates the display layer visually.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_do_layer_update_proc`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_do_layer_update_proc>)
    - [`grect_get_max_y`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#grect_get_max_y>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)


---
### prv\_update\_time<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_time}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L58>)

Updates the time display in the `SdkAppData` structure if the `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` flag is set.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to an `SdkAppData` structure that contains the time buffer and text layer to update.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined.
    - Copies the current time as a string into `data->time_buffer` using `clock_copy_time_string`.
    - Sets the text of `data->time_text_layer` to the value in `data->time_buffer` using [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>).
- **Output**: No return value; updates the `time_buffer` and `time_text_layer` in the `SdkAppData` structure.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)


---
### prv\_update\_info<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_info}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L65>)

Updates the text and background color of the `info_text_layer` based on the last installed app and system capabilities.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to `SdkAppData` structure containing the `info_text_layer` and `window` to update.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the last installed app ID using `shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app()` and store it in `app_id`.
    - Check if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined to determine the control flow path.
    - If `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined, set `tip_delay_s` to 5 seconds.
    - If `app_id` is `INSTALL_ID_INVALID` or the last installed app is a watchface, set `text` to "Install an app to continue".
    - If the current time divided by `tip_delay_s` is odd, set `text` to "Press Select to access Launcher"; otherwise, set `text` to "Press Down to access Timeline".
    - If `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is not defined, check if `app_id` is `INSTALL_ID_INVALID`.
    - If `app_id` is `INSTALL_ID_INVALID`, set `text` to "Install an app to continue or press up / down to browse the timeline" and `color` to `GColorChromeYellow`.
    - If `app_id` is valid, set `text` to "Press select to launch your app or press up / down to browse the timeline" and `color` to `GColorPictonBlue`.
    - Set the window background color using `window_set_background_color()` with `color` or `GColorWhite` if color is not supported.
    - Update the `info_text_layer` text with the selected `text` using `text_layer_set_text()`.
- **Output**: Updates the `info_text_layer` with a message and optionally changes the window background color.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app`](<../../shell/sdk/shell_sdk.c.md#shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app>)
    - [`shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface`](<../../shell/sdk/shell_sdk.c.md#shell_sdk_last_installed_app_is_watchface>)
    - [`window_set_background_color`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_background_color>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)


---
### prv\_update\_ui<!-- {{#callable:prv_update_ui}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L92>)

Updates the user interface by refreshing the time and information displayed.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to `SdkAppData` structure containing UI elements to update.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_update_time`](<#prv_update_time>) with `data` to update the time display.
    - Calls [`prv_update_info`](<#prv_update_info>) with `data` to update the information text display.
- **Output**: No return value; updates are applied directly to the UI elements in `data`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_time`](<#prv_update_time>)
    - [`prv_update_info`](<#prv_update_info>)

### prv\_handle\_tick\_timer<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_tick_timer}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L97>)

Updates the user interface when the tick timer event occurs.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``tick_time``: A pointer to a `struct tm` representing the current time.
    - ``units_changed``: A `TimeUnits` value indicating which time units have changed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls [`prv_update_ui`](<#prv_update_ui>) with the user data obtained from `app_state_get_user_data()`.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_update_ui`](<#prv_update_ui>)

### prv\_launch\_last\_installed\_app<!-- {{#callable:prv_launch_last_installed_app}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L102>)

Launches the last installed application if it is valid.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the click recognizer, used to identify the button press event.
    - ``data``: A pointer to additional data, not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the last installed application's ID using `shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app()` and store it in `app_id`.
    - Log the ID of the last installed application using `PBL_LOG`.
    - Check if `app_id` is not equal to `INSTALL_ID_INVALID`.
    - If valid, create an `AppLaunchEventConfig` with the `app_id` and call `app_manager_put_launch_app_event()` to launch the application.
- **Output**: No direct output; it triggers an event to launch an application if the ID is valid.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app`](<../../shell/sdk/shell_sdk.c.md#shell_sdk_get_last_installed_app>)


---
### prv\_launch\_timeline<!-- {{#callable:prv_launch_timeline}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L112>)

Launches the timeline application in either past or future mode based on the button pressed.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the click recognizer that identifies which button was pressed.
    - ``data``: A pointer to additional data, not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize a static `TimelineArgs` structure with default values for `launch_into_pin` and `pin_id`.
    - Determine if the `BUTTON_ID_UP` button was pressed using `click_recognizer_get_button_id`.
    - If `BUTTON_ID_UP` is pressed, set `s_timeline_args.direction` to `TimelineIterDirectionPast` and log the action.
    - If any other button is pressed, set `s_timeline_args.direction` to `TimelineIterDirectionFuture` and log the action.
    - Call `app_manager_put_launch_app_event` with a configuration to launch the timeline app, passing `s_timeline_args` as arguments.
- **Output**: No direct output; it triggers an event to launch the timeline application.


---
### prv\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:prv_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L134>)

Configures button click event handlers for a window when `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is not defined.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to user-defined data, not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is not defined.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_UP` button to the `prv_launch_timeline` function.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_SELECT` button to the `prv_launch_last_installed_app` function.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_DOWN` button to the `prv_launch_timeline` function.
- **Output**: No return value; the function configures button click handlers.

### prv\_init<!-- {{#callable:prv_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L142>)

Initializes the SDK application window and its components, setting up the user interface and event handling.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for `SdkAppData` and check for successful allocation.
    - Initialize the `Window` object and set its properties such as click configuration, back button override, and fullscreen mode.
    - Set the update procedure for the window's layer to `prv_sdk_home_update_proc`.
    - Determine the frame dimensions for the text layers based on the device capabilities and set the top margin accordingly.
    - If `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined, initialize the `time_text_layer` with specific font and alignment, and adjust the frame for the `info_text_layer`.
    - Initialize the `info_text_layer` with specific font and alignment, and add it as a child to the window's layer.
    - If the device is round, enable text flow and paging for the `info_text_layer` with a specific inset.
    - Set the `SdkAppData` as user data for the application state.
    - If `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined, set the window's background color and subscribe to the tick timer service with `prv_handle_tick_timer`.
    - Call [`prv_update_ui`](<#prv_update_ui>) to update the user interface with the current data.
    - Push the window onto the application window stack with animation.
- **Output**: No output is returned as the function is of type `void`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_click_config_provider`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_click_config_provider>)
    - [`window_set_overrides_back_button`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_overrides_back_button>)
    - [`window_set_fullscreen`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_fullscreen>)
    - [`text_layer_init`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init>)
    - [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text_alignment`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text_alignment>)
    - [`text_layer_set_overflow_mode`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_overflow_mode>)
    - [`text_layer_set_background_color`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_background_color>)
    - [`text_layer_enable_screen_text_flow_and_paging`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_enable_screen_text_flow_and_paging>)
    - [`window_set_background_color`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_background_color>)
    - [`prv_update_ui`](<#prv_update_ui>)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L205>)

Initializes the application and starts the event loop.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`prv_init`](<#prv_init>) function to initialize the application.
    - Enters the `app_event_loop` to handle application events.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`prv_init`](<#prv_init>)

### sdk\_app\_get\_info<!-- {{#callable:sdk_app_get_info}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/sdk/sdk_app.c#L211>)

Returns a pointer to a static `PebbleProcessMdSystem` structure containing metadata about the application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Defines a static `PebbleProcessMdSystem` structure `s_app_md` with application metadata.
    - Sets the `main_func` to `s_main`, which is the main function of the application.
    - Assigns a specific UUID to the application for identification.
    - Conditionally sets the `process_type` to `ProcessTypeWatchface` if `CAPABILITY_HAS_SDK_SHELL4` is defined.
    - Sets the `icon_resource_id` to `RESOURCE_ID_MENU_ICON_TICTOC_WATCH`.
    - Names the application "TicToc".
    - Returns a pointer to the `s_app_md` structure.
- **Output**: A pointer to a `PebbleProcessMd` structure containing the application's metadata.
